title: 〜にもかかわらず
description: 〜にもかかわらず means "despite" or "in spite of." It is a formal, written-sounding concessive: something happens even though the situation would normally prevent or discourage it.

content.text: 〜にもかかわらず attaches to a noun or to a plain-form clause and means the result holds despite it. 雨（あめ）にもかかわらず = "despite the rain"; 努力（どりょく）したにもかかわらず = "despite having made an effort." It belongs to formal writing, speeches, and news rather than casual chat.

content.japanese: Noun ／ Verb・Adjective （plain）　＋ にもかかわらず
雨（あめ）にもかかわらず、試合（しあい）は行（おこな）われた。
content.english: Despite the rain, the match was held.

content.japanese: 努力（どりょく）したにもかかわらず、失敗（しっぱい）した。
content.english: Despite making an effort, I failed.

content.japanese: 忙（いそが）しいにもかかわらず、彼（かれ）は手伝（てつだ）ってくれた。
content.english: Despite being busy, he helped me out.

content.japanese: 何度（なんど）も注意（ちゅうい）したにもかかわらず、彼（かれ）は同（おな）じミスを繰（く）り返（かえ）す。
content.english: Despite being warned many times, he keeps making the same mistake.

content.text: Register: 〜にもかかわらず vs 〜のに

〜のに and 〜にもかかわらず both mean "even though / despite," but 〜のに is everyday spoken Japanese and often carries emotion, while 〜にもかかわらず is formal and objective. In an essay or announcement, 〜にもかかわらず sounds appropriate; in a chat with friends, 〜のに is more natural.

Don't drop the も. 〜にかかわらず (without も) means "regardless of / irrespective of" and pairs with words like 天候（てんこう） or 年齢（ねんれい）: 天候（てんこう）にかかわらず、決行（けっこう）します = "we'll go ahead regardless of the weather." 〜にもかかわらず (with も) means "despite (this specific thing)." Same kanji, different job.

examples.japanese: Noun ／ Verb・Adjective （plain）　＋ にもかかわらず
雨（あめ）にもかかわらず、試合（しあい）は行（おこな）われた。
examples.english: Despite the rain, the match was held.

examples.japanese: 努力（どりょく）したにもかかわらず、失敗（しっぱい）した。
examples.english: Despite making an effort, I failed.

examples.japanese: 忙（いそが）しいにもかかわらず、彼（かれ）は手伝（てつだ）ってくれた。
examples.english: Despite being busy, he helped me out.

examples.japanese: 何度（なんど）も注意（ちゅうい）したにもかかわらず、彼（かれ）は同（おな）じミスを繰（く）り返（かえ）す。
examples.english: Despite being warned many times, he keeps making the same mistake.

headings.text: Register: 〜にもかかわらず vs 〜のに
